health_zone_table.basis: SitRep39 Table 2 (22 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1/province totals 1094/277. No new affected health zone is reported; the affected-zone count remains 34/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. PDF sha256 00d159032158b2955e6be84d9155faadddd140c484ec1f42e44dfab025c4e79f.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1077 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation; all 277 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep39 section 4.3: Ituri 100 collected/analyzed / 43 positive; Nord-Kivu 73 collected, 65 analyzed / 3 positive, with 8 pending analysis. Positivity uses 46 positives over 165 analyzed samples. Sud-Kivu reported no laboratory line this cycle.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 387 patients (190 confirmed, 197 suspected) at 76.8% occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 87.0% with 321 patients isolated at end day; Nord-Kivu was 54.4% with 56 and Sud-Kivu 31.3% with 10.
One suspect/confirmed patient escaped during the cycle; 3 recovered exits were recorded.
highlights: 46 new confirmed cases including 10 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-22.
New confirmed cases were reported in Ituri (43 including 10 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (3 including 0 deaths), led by Bunia (11), Rwampara (10), and Mongbwalu (8).
3 patients were declared recovered after negative control tests, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 115.
laboratory: SitRep39 reports Ituri 100 samples collected and analyzed with 43 positive (43.0%).
Nord-Kivu collected 73 samples, analyzed 65, with 3 positive (4.6%) and 8 pending analysis; Sud-Kivu reported no laboratory line this cycle.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 39 reports through 2026-06-22 and was published on the INSP WordPress site on 2026-06-23T21:45:09Z (post 25044).
The outbreak remains reported across 3 provinces and 34 of 104 health zones; no new affected health zone is reported in this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 997 of 1094 DRC confirmed cases (91.1%).
Nord-Kivu stands at 94 confirmed cases and keeps the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 53.2%, led by Oicha (66.7%), Beni (60.0%), and Katwa (59.4%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 891 alerts were reported and 877 investigated, producing a 98.4% investigation rate; Sud-Kivu alerts were not investigated this cycle due to a fuel shortage.
131 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 44 deceased validated alerts.
8,278 contacts were under follow-up and 6,023 were seen, producing 72.8% follow-up coverage; Nord-Kivu follow-up (71.6%) is computed on 7 of 10 reporting zones.
519 new contacts were listed, including 411 across seven Ituri health zones and 108 across five Nord-Kivu zones.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Sud-Kivu alerts were not investigated this cycle due to a fuel shortage; 519 new contacts were listed (411 across seven Ituri zones and 108 across five Nord-Kivu zones).

challenges.finding: Ituri CTE saturation reached 87.0% with an estimated gap of about 20 isolation centers.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction and expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 72.8% versus the 95% target (a 22.2 pp gap).
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet precisely ventilated by health zone.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, duplicate cleanup, field verification, and reclassification.

challenges.finding: SitRep39 challenge table reports 78 health-worker infections and 18 deaths (CFR 23.1%).
challenges.response: Reinforce IPC/WASH supplies (PPE, chlorine), supervision, and training.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu carries a laboratory backlog of 8 pending samples and late diagnosis amid a 53.2% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 519 new contacts were listed (411 across seven Ituri zones and 108 across five Nord-Kivu zones); Nord-Kivu follow-up is computed on 7 of 10 reporting zones (70.0% completeness).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Protect Ituri isolation capacity as occupancy stays high (87.0%) and close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (72.8% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog (8 pending) given the 53.2% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep39 headline is DRC-only (1094 confirmed / 277 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.
SitRep39 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
Daily suspected cases (131) and the 44 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.
The confirmed-count banner carries an asterisk noting DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization and consolidation.
Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries, carried forward unchanged from SitRep38 (INSP reports DRC only).

review_notes: SitRep #039 (report date 2026-06-22; INSP WordPress post 25044 published 2026-06-23T21:45:09Z; embedded PDF media 25045). PDF sha256 00d159032158b2955e6be84d9155faadddd140c484ec1f42e44dfab025c4e79f, 11 pages. NOTE: INSP post slug/filename mislabel it 'N°37/20-06' (CMS bug); the PDF body is unambiguously SitRep N°39 for 22 June 2026.
DRC headline 1094 confirmed / 277 confirmed deaths; +46 confirmed and +10 confirmed deaths reported for 2026-06-22 (Ituri 43/10, Nord-Kivu 3/0).
The affected-zone count remains 34/104. No new affected health zone is reported, so no new map geometry/centroid is required this cycle.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1094 confirmed / 277 deaths: 1077 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with 277 named confirmed deaths and no death residual.

Recovered advanced to 115 DRC cumulative (+3). Uganda anchor unchanged at 20 cumulative cases (per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard) with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries, so country-scope is 1114 confirmed / 279 deaths / 126 recovered.
Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 100 collected/analyzed, 43 positive; Nord-Kivu 73 collected, 65 analyzed, 3 positive, 8 pending. Sud-Kivu reported no lab line this cycle.

Operational data captured: 891 alerts (877 investigated, 98.4%; Sud-Kivu uninvestigated for lack of fuel), 131 suspected cases of the day (44 deceased validated alerts), 8,278 contacts under follow-up (72.8%; Nord-Kivu on 7/10 reporting zones), 387 in isolation (190 confirmed / 197 suspected, 76.8% occupancy), 519 new contacts (411 Ituri / 108 Nord-Kivu), PoC activation 86.8% (59/68) with 130,923 travelers passed.

Geographic note: Nord-Kivu provincial CFR 53.2% remains the highest, led by Oicha (66.7%), Beni (60.0%), Katwa (59.4%); Mongbwalu severity persists at 42.2% (100/237). INSP flags continued progression and possible rapid geographic expansion. Promoted as table facts only.
